inputs.label: Paint Volume
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of paint to be used. Based on manufacturer specifications and ASTM D2697.

inputs.label: Coverage Rate
inputs.businessContext: Typical coverage per liter; varies by paint type and surface. Refer to ISO 8501 for surface preparation standards.

inputs.label: Number of Coats
inputs.businessContext: Number of coats applied. Industry standard for most applications is 2 coats.

inputs.label: Surface Area
inputs.businessContext: Total area to be painted. Measured per project specifications.

inputs.label: Waste Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of paint wasted due to overspray, spillage, etc. Typical range 5-15% based on Lean manufacturing principles.

inputs.label: Paint Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of paint affects coverage rate and application method. Refer to ASTM D5146 for paint testing.

inputs.label: Surface Condition
inputs.businessContext: Surface texture impacts paint absorption and coverage. Based on ISO 8501 surface preparation grades.

inputs.label: Application Method
inputs.businessContext: Method affects transfer efficiency and waste. Spray has higher waste; brush/roller more efficient.

thresholds.wasteFactor > 20: High waste factor; consider improving application technique.
thresholds.coverageRate < 5: Low coverage rate; check paint quality or surface preparation.
thresholds.numberOfCoats > 3: Multiple coats may indicate poor coverage; consider primer.

suggestedActions: if wasteFactor > 20 then 'Use spray shield or improve technique'
if coverageRate < 5 then 'Consider primer or surface preparation'
if numberOfCoats > 3 then 'Use high-hide paint or primer'
